purpose: Cyrus product workflow contract cases: each sub skill gets one happy path and one corner case, then the confirmed end-to-end flow is checked.

cases.objective: Parse an existing H5 deck into a source dossier with knowledge, material, slide layers, and downstream handoff targets.
expected: HTML dependencies are preserved as material items

cases.objective: A missing local source must not crash the parser when --allow-missing is used, and must surface confirmation gaps.
A production-ready outline validates with the stricter Cyrus design contract.
expected: every slide has hero, density_budget, design_spec, content_completion, and fact_boundary

cases.objective: A thin page-list outline must fail the strict design gate before it reaches renderer.
cases.input: rich outline fixture with one design field removed
expected: failure points to the missing fine-grained design field

cases.objective: Compile a confirmed outline to DeckJSON, validate it, and render index.html/texts.md/FEEDBACK.md.
Invalid DeckJSON is rejected before rendering.
cases.input: bad deck.json with invalid key and missing content variant

cases.objective: Auditor wraps H5 check-only and emits a valid audit-report.json for a rendered deck.
Broken standalone HTML must be rejected and routed away from ingestion.
Simulate a post-audit pitch rehearsal from outline + deck.json.
expected: audience_panel has at least three roles

cases.objective: Unsupported meeting types are rejected by the simulator CLI instead of silently normalizing unintended input.
Audited decks can produce a schema-valid dry-run ingestion manifest without writing local or cloud libraries.
Unaudited deck output is blocked from ingestion by default.
The confirmed pipeline can render, audit, rehearse, and emit the fixed handoff artifacts offline.
Brief-only generator tasks must pause after DESIGN_PLAN/outline and must not render before user outline confirmation.
Non-interactive auto-confirm is blocked unless the caller explicitly opts into skipping outline confirmation.
After the generated deck passes audit and rehearsal, accepting rehearsal feedback publishes the Magic Page dry-run and pauses for ingestion confirmation.
When the user chooses to modify based on pitch simulator feedback, the workflow creates a new version and returns to outline confirmation instead of mutating the rendered deck directly.
If the user declines ingestion after the delivery is ready, the task closes without writing ingestion records.
High-stakes manufacturing AI decks are blocked from publish when rehearsal asks for more evidence.
cases.input: synthetic manufacturing AI rehearsal with low trust and P0 evidence item
expected: blockers explain evidence or trust issue
